summary: Tested WeHA full-stack (backend audit-requests API + frontend 5-page React site). All 6 pytest backend tests passed (CRUD + 422 validation + GET listing). Frontend E2E via Playwright: all 5 nav links work with teal active underline, theme toggle adds/removes 'dark' class on documentElement, FAQ accordion expands, contact form validates empty submit (toast) and successfully submits when filled (success state shown and verified persisted via GET /api/audit-requests). Mobile view at 375px: hamburger opens full-screen menu, all nav items visible, no horizontal overflow. Three.js hero-sphere renders without console errors. No console errors observed during any flow.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown'); consider lifespan handlers (minor, not blocking).
POST /api/audit-requests returns 200 on create — convention would be 201. Not breaking, but worth noting.
AuditRequestCreate has no length/format constraints on company/country/industry/contact_method/email; only name+process are validated. EmailStr is imported but not used on the email field.
Contact form sends 'email' as optional string but UI accepts free text — no client-side email validation beyond HTML type=email.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed audit-request records via API and E2E form submission (3 entries currently in DB, all prefixed for easy cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file is /app/backend/tests/test_audit_requests.py and uses pytest with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL fallback to frontend/.env. The /api/audit-requests POST currently returns 200 (not 201). Empty name and empty (or whitespace-only) process both return 422 as expected. Frontend data-testids are well-populated and reliable. Three.js hero renders cleanly. All five routes (Home, Services, Work, About, Contact) load via header nav; mobile menu uses opacity+pointer-events for show/hide (not display:none).
